steps.title: Locate the angle
steps.content:
The angle $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ equals $120^\circ$, so it is in quadrant II. Its reference angle is
$$
\pi-\frac{2\pi}{3}=\frac{\pi}{3}
$$

steps.title: Use the sine value
steps.content: The sine of the reference angle is $$ \sin\bigl(\frac{\pi}{3}\bigr)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$ Sine is positive in quadrant II, so $$ \sin\bigl(\frac{2\pi}{3}\bigr)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$ The answer is D.
